This dialog allows you to upload a custom SSL certificate for HTTPS access, or generate a self-signed SSL certificate.

Inception's web interface can be configured to allow access via HTTPS for a greater level of security (compared to standard HTTP) when configuring the system. HTTPS access can be enabled either in combination with HTTP access, or with HTTP disabled for the highest level of security and encryption. When HTTP access is disabled, plain HTTP requests made to the Inception controller will be redirected to use the HTTPS protocol instead.

HTTPS access for Inception is enabled by default with an auto-generated SSL certificate which is self-signed by the Inception controller, you can choose to keep the default SSL certificate or upload your own custom SSL certificate and private key.

Choosing to use a self-signed certificate will provide encryption on web traffic, but may cause your browser to display a warning that "the connection is not private". The self-signed certificate can be manually imported into your browser's trusted certificate store, but for more permanent situations, it is recommended that a trusted custom SSL certificate is installed by your network administrator.
If the new configuration cannot be applied (e.g. due to an invalid certificate or password), the existing HTTPS configuration and certificate will be left unmodified.
It is highly recommended that you perform a database backup before modifying the HTTP/S configuration in case it affects your access to the web interface.
This dialog allows you to upload a custom SSL certificate for HTTPS access.

When uploading a custom SSL certificate, you should have a PEM-formatted certificate file (.crt or .pem) and the accompanying PEM-formatted key file (.key). If your certificate is in a different file format (e.g. .p12/.pfx), you will first need to convert it to PEM format using an external utility such as
Javascript must be enabled to interact with this webpage. Please turn it before trying again.
This browser is not supported with this version of Inception.
Note that since Inception version 2.0, Internet Explorer is no longer a supported browser. If using Windows 10, the Microsoft Edge browser is still supported.
Otherwise, please try another browser, such as
Doors can be scheduled to automatically unlock and lock based on a time period or whether an area on one side of the door is armed or not.
To configure when a door will automatically lock and unlock, select a time period from the
drop-down or an area from the

- When the time period goes valid (the current time is inside of the specified times) the door will unlock and when the time period goes invalid (the current time is outside of the specified times) the door will lock.
This is commonly used when an internal door needs to be in free access during working hours.
Time periods are programmed and edited on the
- When the area is armed the door will automatically lock, however, disarming the area will not unlock the door.
This is commonly used when you need to ensure a door is locked when the area on the inside of the door is armed.

Both options can be combined to result in a door that will only unlock when both the time period is valid and the area is disarmed. The door will relock when the time period goes invalid or the area re-arms. This allows a door to be normally unlocked during the day, but only after someone with authorization to disarm the area is there.
To configure when an area will automatically arm, select a time period from the

The area will automatically arm when the selected time period becomes active (the current time is inside of the specified times). This feature is commonly used to automatically arm the area late at night to ensure that the area is armed over night in case a user forgets to arm it.
By default, the area will not automatically disarm when the time period goes invalid, however this can be changed with the
Lift Floors can be scheduled to be set to free access or secured based on a time period or whether the area on that floor is armed or not.
To configure when a lift floor will automatically be set to free access, select a time period from the
drop-down and set whether the floor should also follow the area on that floors state.

- When the time period goes valid (the current time is inside of the specified times) the floor will be set to free access and when the time period goes invalid (the current time is outside of the specified times) the floor will secure. A floor in free access allows any user to select that floor in the lift car, while a secured floor requires the user to present a credential to allow them to select that floor.
This is commonly used when a common floor needs unrestricted access at certain times of the day.
- When the area is armed the floor will automatically secure, however, disarming the area will not set the floor to free access.
This is commonly used when you need to ensure a floor is secured when the area on that floor is armed, restricting access to only those who can disarm the area.

Both options can be combined to result in a floor that will only be in free access when both the time period is valid and the area is disarmed. The floor will re-secure when the time period goes invalid or the area re-arms. This allows a floor to be normally free to all users during the day, but only after someone with authorization to disarm the area is there.

Clicking Manage Languages will open the Manage Languages menu, where you can upload, delete and apply Language Packs. Clicking the Download Translation Template button will download a translation template file to use with the Inception Translation Tool.

The Inception web interface is capable of being displayed in different languages through the usage of Inception Language Pack (.irlanguage) files. These files can either be supplied by Inner Range or the installer, or manually created and edited with the Inception Translation Tool, a separate stand-alone application. To create a language pack, the first step is to download a copy of the Inception System's language pack template file to import into the Inception Translation Tool. This includes all of the translatable text in the Inception system for the current firmware version.

You can download a language pack template file by clicking the Download Translation Template button from the Manage Languages dialog. You can import this template file into the Inception Translation Tool to generate a new translation project. Or, it can be merged with an older translation project to translate only the text that has been added in the new firmware since a translation was last done.

After you've finished the translation process using the Inception Translation Tool, you can export a Language Pack file (.irlanguage) and then upload it to the Inception controller from a PC. To upload a language pack, click the Manage Languages button, then click the Upload button and choose the file on disk that you want to upload. Upon being uploaded successfully, the language pack should appear in the list of available languages in the Manage Languages dialog box. From here, you can select the desired language from the list and click Apply in order to change the current language of the Inception controller (this action requires rebooting the device).

If uploading the language pack fails, it could mean that the language pack file data was exported incorrectly, or that the file has been corrupted at some point during transfer. Try re-exporting the language pack from the Inception Translation Tool if possible and re-uploading it.

The LAN Encryption Key Changeover mode is intended to help migrate Ethernet LAN modules (e.g. Ethernet Bridge) from an old encryption key to the current key configured in Inception. All Ethernet LAN modules in your system must be configured with the same encryption key as the controller in order for them to be able to communicate.

The LAN Key Changeover mode puts the Inception controller in a state where it will temporarily accept Ethernet LAN messages encrypted with an alternate specified key (or the default key), for a period of 2 minutes. When Inception receives a connection request from a module using the alternate key, it will automatically migrate that module to the current key.

This mode can be useful in the event where you have changed the Inception's LAN encryption key but a module was offline and did not receive a key change message, or if you have connected a new, unconfigured Ethernet LAN module and need to change its key so it can be enrolled.

Some System Warnings and Messages can only be cleared by a user with special permissions, which means that LCD Terminals may keep beeping periodically and flashing LEDs until the warnings/messages are cleared. If the fault cannot be fixed right away, the "Mute" button on this dialog can stop the periodic beeping until the warning/message can be attended to. (Note: New Messages/Warnings will cause the LCD Terminal to start to draw attention again.)

The calendar preview below is used to check that the time period is programmed correctly.

The calendar preview can be used to view any date in the future and will display when the time period is valid on that day, taking into account any calendar exceptions or date & time exceptions. This is useful to confirm whether exceptions planned for the future have been applied correctly.
When changes are made to the time period or the exceptions, it is recommended to update the calendar preview first by clicking the

. The calendar can be displayed in days, weeks or months by pressing the appropriate button. Depending on the selected display mode you can advance the calendar forward and backwards by pressing the arrow keys.
The green colour indicates times that the time period will be active and white indicates the invalid times. Certain times may be red, which indicates times that are invalid due to exceptions.
Enter the email address of this user to send the invitation code to. By default, this field autofills to the selected user's
A mobile credential will be generated and assigned to this user with the status of
. An invitation code for this mobile credential will then be sent to the user this linked mobile credential is linked to, using the specified email address.
Opens the export CSV file menu to export user information into a .csv file. The menu will have user fields that can be selected to filter down the exported user data.
Opens the import CSV file menu to import a .csv file that will update existing users and create new ones based on the names of the users specified in the .csv file.

Opens a menu that allows for the import of user photos in bulk through uploading a ZIP file with JPEG image files. The image files should be named to the corresponding user the photo will be assigned to.

Import user photos in bulk by uploading a ZIP file containing multiple JPEG image files inside. Image filenames are matched up with users based on the user's Name, so it is recommended that user names are unique.

The uploaded ZIP file should contain only JPEG (.jpg/.jpeg) image files whose filenames match the names of users in the Inception system. Files which are not JPEG files or files whose names do not match a user in the system will be ignored. All images should be placed in the main directory of the ZIP folder, not in subfolders. The uploaded zip file cannot exceed 100MB, larger batches of images may have to be split into multiple zip files.

The maximum supported image size is 480 x 640px (3:4 aspect ratio), images will be scaled down if they exceed these dimensions, so it is recommended that user photos are uploaded with a similar resolution and aspect ratio for optimal quality.

Import users in bulk by uploading a CSV file. Users can either be added or updated by matching on the user Name in the CSV. As updating of users is keyed on Name, it is recommended that user names are unique.
. Add the user information to the spreadsheet, save it as a CSV file and upload it in this menu.

The CSV file must contain a header line that specifies the User data columns that are to be imported, and the column titles must match the User properties as they appear on the Manage User page. The "Name" property is a mandatory field and must be included as one of the header line columns, all other fields are optional.
An example of a valid header column format:
Each data row must have at least the Name column filled in.

Entries for the "Permissions" field are only allowed to be the name of an existing Permission Group in the system (case-insensitive); user permissions for individual items cannot be assigned through CSV import.
Entries for the "Credentials" and "Remote Fobs" fields should contain only the Credential/Fob number; the Credential Template and Fob Template are specified below in the import options.
Note that Mobile Credentials are unable to be imported via CSV.
Entries for checkbox fields (e.g. "Use Extended Unlock Times") will only accept the words "true" or "false" (case-insensitive).

Fields consisting of lists of items (e.g. Credentials, Remote Fob, Permissions) can only accept a single item at a time due to the limitations of the CSV format. For newly imported Users, the single item will become the only item in the list field. For existing users, the single item will be appended to the existing list of items (if it is not already present). In the case where you wish to import a User(s) with 2 credentials assigned to them, for example: you can CSV import the User(s) initially with a single credential number specified in the Credentials field, and then perform another CSV import with the same Users' names but with the 2nd credential number in the Credentials field, and the existing User will gain the 2nd credential number in their Credentials list.

Fields that refer to other items in the system (e.g. LCD Terminal Profile, Permission Groups, Web Page Profile) will be linked up based on the item's Name field (case-insensitive). If no item is found with the specified item name, the import will fail.

Fields with drop-down lists of options (e.g. When to Cancel User) will only accept values that match the text options in the drop-down list (e.g. "First Card Or PIN"). The values are case-insensitive.

The recommended format for date/time field data (e.g. User Expiry Time) is "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS". This avoids any ambiguity with the date format. Alternate date formats may be accepted, but through testing should be done first to confirm and will depend on the selected system language.

The "Encrypted Security PIN" and "Encrypted Password" fields will only accept encrypted data strings that have been exported via the Export User CSV dialog or from the Inception REST API. This option is provided to allow cloning User data from one Inception system to another without having to restore an entire database backup, as plain-text PINs and passwords cannot be exported from Inception for security reasons. To input plain-text PINs or passwords in the CSV import, use the "Security PIN" or "Password" fields instead. Avoid using the encrypted and unencrypted variants of the same input field when entering user data.

When using CSV Import to update users, if no value is specified for a field then no change is made. This means that, for example; to update an email address for a user, no permission group, PIN, credential, or notes need to be specified.
Existing user data fields can also be erased by inputting \

" for the specific field. This is different to not specifying a value for a field, which will leave the field unchanged for the user. This only works for fields that are allowed to be empty (e.g. Notes, Credentials); fields that must reference another item (Web Page Profile, LCD Terminal Profile) cannot be set to blank and will cause the import to fail.

If the import fails the process will abort and no users will be created. However if the import was being used to update users, any updates that occurred before the failure will be persisted in the system.
Some causes of import failures are:
One or more of the users don't have a name.
The specified item name (Permission Group, Web Page Profile, etc.) name could not be found in the system.
A PIN provided already matches an existing User or there are duplicate PINs in the import file.
A credential provided already matches an existing User or there are duplicate credentials in the import file.
A supplied user data field was not in a valid format (Email Address, User Expiry Time, etc.)
Choose the fields that you want to be included in the User CSV Export data.

Due to security reasons, the "Security PIN" and "Password" fields will not export any sensitive data to the CSV file if they are included (the fields will be left blank), however they can still be included in the CSV output in order to use the exported CSV as a template to be filled in for importing users.

If you wish to export user PINs or passwords from a system and import them into another Inception system, select the "Encrypted Security PIN" and/or the "Encrypted Password" fields instead. These fields will contain an encrypted version of the data which is only readable by Inception systems, and can be imported into other systems with the sensitive data intact.
Inception supports Two-Factor Authentication (2FA) with a smartphone or tablet using the Google Authenticator app or another compatible Time-based One-time Password (TOTP) authenticator app.
Using your authenticator app, scan the QR code using your smartphone's camera, or manually enter the authentication key to add a new 2FA account.
Once you have created an account, enter the 6-digit code from the authenticator app and click the Enrol button to confirm your 2FA enrolment.
Search user data by entering text in the

text box will search all visible user data fields and filter the table results to display only the users whose details contain a partial or exact match for the search text. Note that hidden fields are not included in the search results, you will need to make them visible by using the
button if you want to search the contents of those fields. The
text box can also be be used in combination with the individual column search filters in order to filter the results to a finer degree.
This dialog allows you to view the selected Integrated Connection's current trusted certificate, and match the stored trusted fingerprint to the connection's fingerprint if it does not match.

Warning: Two or more readers connected to the selected module share the same OSDP address. The set address command uses the original addresses to find the target reader, so duplicate addresses may result in unintended consequences when setting up new addresses. Unplugging duplicate readers until a unique address is configured is recommended.

Are you sure you want to delete this module?

Are you sure you want to reset this controller's IO mode? This allows you to switch the controller between using simple inputs and outputs and being able to configure readers and doors onboard.
This will not affect any other items in the system or any inputs and outputs linked to other hardware.
Note, any previously configured inputs, outputs, doors and readers connected directly to the controller itself will need to be reprogrammed.
